subject: Statistics
title: Bayes' Theorem: The Formula
description: A fast breakdown of Bayes' Theorem: The Formula for builders.

hook.text: One formula connects prior belief, evidence, and updated belief. It's elegant, powerful, and 250 years old.

buildup.text: P(H|E) = P(E|H) × P(H) / P(E). Posterior equals likelihood times prior, divided by evidence.

discovery.text: Each part has a name: prior is your starting belief, likelihood is how well the evidence fits, posterior is your update.

twist.text: The formula is simple. Applying it to real problems is hard — because estimating priors is genuinely difficult.

climax.text: Bayes' theorem isn't just math. It's a recipe for rational thinking in an uncertain world.

punchline.text: Prior × Likelihood = Posterior. Update your beliefs with math.

quiz.question: In Bayes' theorem, what does the 'posterior' represent?
options: Your updated belief after incorporating new evidence
Your initial belief before seeing evidence
The probability of the evidence occurring
